subtopic: Enthalpy changes, (ΔH), in physical and chemical processes
question: The symbol ΔH represents the

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the meaning of the symbol ΔH in chemistry.
explanation.principle: The Greek letter delta, Δ, means change, while H stands for enthalpy.
steps: Enthalpy is the heat content of a system at constant pressure.
When a process occurs, the enthalpy may increase or decrease.
The symbol used for that change is ΔH.
Therefore ΔH means change in enthalpy.
explanation.simple_explanation: Δ means change, so ΔH simply means change in enthalpy. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Enthalpy changes, (ΔH), in physical and chemical processes
question: For an exothermic reaction, the value of ΔH is usually

explanation.question_restate: We are to state the sign of ΔH for an exothermic reaction.
explanation.principle: An exothermic reaction gives out heat to the surroundings.
steps: When heat leaves the system, the enthalpy of the system decreases.
A decrease in enthalpy is represented by a negative value of ΔH.
So exothermic processes have ΔH less than zero.
Therefore the sign is negative.
explanation.simple_explanation: Exothermic means heat is released, so ΔH is negative. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Enthalpy changes, (ΔH), in physical and chemical processes
question: For an endothermic process, the value of ΔH is

explanation.question_restate: We are to state the sign of ΔH for an endothermic process.
explanation.principle: Endothermic processes absorb heat from the surroundings.
steps: When heat is absorbed, the system gains enthalpy.
A gain in enthalpy is shown by a positive value of ΔH.
So endothermic changes have ΔH greater than zero.
Therefore the correct sign is positive.
explanation.simple_explanation: Endothermic means heat is taken in, so ΔH is positive. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Which of the following is usually an exothermic process?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify a process that releases heat.
explanation.principle: Exothermic processes give out heat to the surroundings.
steps: Melting and boiling require heat input, so they are endothermic.
Dissolving ammonium chloride in water is also commonly endothermic.
Combustion of methane releases a large amount of heat.
Therefore burning of methane is exothermic.
explanation.simple_explanation: Combustion reactions usually release heat, so they are exothermic. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Which of the following is an endothermic process?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify a process that absorbs heat.
explanation.principle: Endothermic processes take in heat from the surroundings.
steps: Freezing releases heat, so it is exothermic.
Neutralization and combustion are also typically exothermic.
Ammonium chloride dissolving in water causes cooling because heat is absorbed.
Therefore dissolution of NH₄Cl in water is endothermic.
explanation.final_answer: C. dissolution of NH₄Cl in water
explanation.simple_explanation: If the surroundings become cooler, the process is taking in heat and is endothermic. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: When sodium hydroxide dissolves in water, the temperature of the solution usually

options.A: falls because the process is endothermic
options.B: rises because the process is exothermic
options.D: falls because NaOH is a gas

answer_text: rises because the process is exothermic

explanation.question_restate: We are to predict the temperature change when NaOH dissolves in water.
explanation.principle: Dissolution of sodium hydroxide in water releases heat.
steps: As NaOH dissolves, ions separate and interact strongly with water molecules.
This process gives out heat overall.
Because heat is released, the solution temperature rises.
Therefore the dissolution is exothermic and the temperature increases.
explanation.final_answer: B. rises because the process is exothermic
explanation.simple_explanation: Dissolving NaOH warms the solution because heat is released. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: The reaction of sodium metal with water is best described as

explanation.question_restate: We are to describe the nature of sodium reacting with water.
explanation.principle: Sodium is a very reactive Group I metal, and its reaction with water releases heat.
steps: When sodium is placed on water, it reacts quickly to form sodium hydroxide and hydrogen.
A lot of energy is released during the reaction.
Because heat is given out, the reaction is exothermic.
Therefore it is vigorous and exothermic.
explanation.simple_explanation: Sodium reacts quickly with water and releases enough heat to make the reaction vigorous. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Why does potassium react with water more violently than sodium?

options.B: Because potassium is higher in Group I and loses its valence electron more easily

answer_text: Because potassium is higher in Group I and loses its valence electron more easily

explanation.question_restate: We are to explain why potassium reacts more vigorously with water than sodium.
explanation.principle: Reactivity of Group I metals increases down the group.
steps: Potassium is below sodium in Group I.
The outer electron of potassium is farther from the nucleus and is held less strongly.
So potassium loses that electron more easily and reacts more readily.
Therefore potassium reacts with water more violently than sodium.
explanation.final_answer: B. Because potassium is higher in Group I and loses its valence electron more easily
explanation.simple_explanation: Down Group I, metals react more strongly because they lose their outer electron more easily. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: When NH₄Cl dissolves in water, the beaker often feels

options.C: unchanged because no energy change occurs

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the temperature effect when ammonium chloride dissolves in water.
explanation.principle: Dissolution of ammonium chloride is a common example of an endothermic process.
steps: The dissolving process absorbs heat from the water and surroundings.
As heat is taken in, the solution temperature drops.
That is why the beaker feels cooler to the touch.
So the beaker feels cooler because heat is absorbed.
explanation.final_answer: B. cooler because heat is absorbed
explanation.simple_explanation: Ammonium chloride cools the solution because its dissolution is endothermic. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Entropy is best described as a measure of

explanation.question_restate: We are to define entropy in a simple way.
explanation.principle: At this level, entropy is commonly explained as a measure of disorder or randomness in a system.
steps: A highly ordered arrangement has lower entropy.
A more scattered or random arrangement has higher entropy.
This idea helps explain why gases usually have higher entropy than solids.
Therefore entropy is a measure of disorder or randomness.
explanation.final_answer: B. degree of disorder or randomness
explanation.simple_explanation: Entropy tells us how ordered or disordered a system is. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Which state of matter generally has the highest entropy?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the state of matter with the greatest disorder.
explanation.principle: Particles in gases are much more free to move than those in liquids or solids.
steps: In solids, particles are closely packed in fixed positions.
In liquids, particles can move but are still fairly close together.
In gases, particles move freely and are widely spread out.
So gases generally have the highest entropy.
explanation.simple_explanation: Gases are the most disordered common state of matter, so they have the highest entropy. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: When two different gases mix spontaneously, the entropy of the system generally

explanation.question_restate: We are to determine what happens to entropy when gases mix.
explanation.principle: Mixing increases randomness because particles become more spread out and less ordered.
steps: Before mixing, each gas is confined to its own region.
After mixing, the particles are distributed more randomly throughout the available space.
This greater randomness means greater entropy.
Therefore the entropy increases.
explanation.simple_explanation: Mixing gases creates more disorder, so entropy increases. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: The dissolution of a salt is exothermic if

options.B: heat is absorbed from the surroundings
options.C: heat is released to the surroundings

answer_text: heat is released to the surroundings

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the condition that makes salt dissolution exothermic.
explanation.principle: An exothermic process releases heat to the surroundings.
steps: If a salt dissolves and the surrounding solution becomes warmer, heat has been released.
That means the dissolution process is exothermic.
The key sign is release of heat to the surroundings.
Therefore exothermic dissolution means heat is released.
explanation.final_answer: C. heat is released to the surroundings
explanation.simple_explanation: If a dissolving salt makes the solution warmer, the process is exothermic. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: A spontaneous reaction is one that

options.B: occurs without continuous external assistance under the given conditions

answer_text: occurs without continuous external assistance under the given conditions

explanation.question_restate: We are to define a spontaneous reaction correctly.
explanation.principle: Spontaneous means thermodynamically favorable under the stated conditions, not necessarily fast.
steps: A spontaneous process can occur on its own once conditions are suitable.
It may still be slow if the activation energy is high.
So spontaneity is not the same thing as speed.
Therefore a spontaneous reaction is one that occurs without continuous outside help under the given conditions.
explanation.final_answer: B. occurs without continuous external assistance under the given conditions
explanation.simple_explanation: Spontaneous means the change is naturally favorable, though it may still happen slowly. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Which statement is correct about a spontaneous reaction?

options.C: It may be slow even though it is thermodynamically favorable

answer_text: It may be slow even though it is thermodynamically favorable

explanation.question_restate: We are to choose the correct statement about spontaneous processes.
explanation.principle: Spontaneity is controlled by thermodynamics, while speed is controlled by kinetics.
steps: A reaction may be thermodynamically favorable but still have a large activation energy.
That large barrier can make it proceed very slowly.
So a spontaneous reaction does not have to be fast or explosive.
Therefore it may be slow even though it is thermodynamically favorable.
explanation.final_answer: C. It may be slow even though it is thermodynamically favorable
explanation.simple_explanation: A reaction can be spontaneous and still slow because spontaneity and rate are not the same thing. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: At equilibrium, the standard Gibbs free energy change, ΔG°, is

explanation.question_restate: We are to state the value of ΔG° at equilibrium.
explanation.principle: At equilibrium, there is no net driving force for change in either direction under standard thermodynamic interpretation in this topic.
steps: Gibbs free energy combines enthalpy and entropy effects into one quantity.
When a system is at equilibrium, the condition given in this syllabus is ΔG° = 0.
That means there is no net tendency for further spontaneous change in one direction.
Therefore ΔG° is zero at equilibrium.
explanation.simple_explanation: At equilibrium, the system has no net thermodynamic push in either direction, so ΔG° is zero. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: For a spontaneous change, the sign of ΔG° is

explanation.question_restate: We are to state the sign of Gibbs free energy for a spontaneous change.
explanation.principle: A negative Gibbs free energy means the process is thermodynamically favorable.
steps: When ΔG° is less than zero, the process can occur spontaneously under the stated conditions.
This is one of the key rules for Gibbs free energy.
If ΔG° is greater than zero, the change is non-spontaneous.
Therefore a spontaneous change has negative ΔG°.
explanation.simple_explanation: Spontaneous change means ΔG° is less than zero. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: For a non-spontaneous change, ΔG° is

explanation.question_restate: We are to state the sign of ΔG° for a non-spontaneous change.
explanation.principle: A positive Gibbs free energy means the process is not thermodynamically favorable under the given conditions.
steps: If ΔG° is greater than zero, the process does not occur spontaneously.
External energy or different conditions would be needed to make it proceed.
This is the opposite of the spontaneous case.
Therefore a non-spontaneous change has ΔG° greater than zero.
explanation.simple_explanation: Non-spontaneous change means ΔG° is positive. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Given ΔG = ΔH − TΔS, which factor can make a reaction with positive ΔH become spontaneous at high temperature?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify what can make an endothermic reaction become spontaneous at high temperature.
explanation.principle: In the Gibbs equation, the term TΔS becomes more important as temperature increases.
steps: If ΔH is positive, the reaction is endothermic and this alone does not favor spontaneity.
But if ΔS is also positive, the term −TΔS becomes negative.
At high temperature, that negative term can become large enough to outweigh the positive ΔH.
Therefore a large positive ΔS can make the reaction spontaneous at high temperature.
explanation.simple_explanation: A positive entropy change helps high temperature make ΔG negative. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: If ΔH = +40 kJ mol⁻¹ and TΔS = +60 kJ mol⁻¹, then ΔG is

explanation.question_restate: We are to calculate ΔG from the Gibbs equation.
explanation.principle: Use the relation ΔG = ΔH − TΔS.
steps: Substitute the given values directly.
This gives −20 kJ mol⁻¹.
Therefore the Gibbs free energy is −20 kJ mol⁻¹.
explanation.simple_explanation: Just subtract TΔS from ΔH. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: A reaction with ΔG° = −15 kJ mol⁻¹ is

explanation.question_restate: We are to classify a reaction using the sign of ΔG°.
explanation.principle: A negative value of Gibbs free energy indicates a spontaneous change.
steps: The given value is −15 kJ mol⁻¹.
Because it is less than zero, the change is thermodynamically favorable.
So the reaction can proceed spontaneously under the stated conditions.
Therefore it is spontaneous.
explanation.simple_explanation: A negative ΔG° means the change is spontaneous. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Which of the following changes is most likely to increase entropy?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify a change that increases disorder most strongly.
explanation.principle: Entropy usually increases when matter changes from a more ordered state to a less ordered one.
steps: Freezing, condensation and crystallization all move toward more order.
Sublimation changes a solid directly into gas.
Gas particles are much more randomly arranged than solid particles.
Therefore sublimation of iodine increases entropy.
explanation.simple_explanation: Changing from solid to gas greatly increases disorder, so entropy rises. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Why is mixing of gases usually spontaneous?

options.C: Because gases become solids after mixing

explanation.question_restate: We are to explain why gases usually mix on their own.
explanation.principle: Spontaneous mixing is strongly favored by the increase in randomness that results.
steps: Before mixing, each gas occupies its own region.
After mixing, the gas particles are more widely distributed and more disordered.
This means entropy increases.
Therefore mixing of gases is usually spontaneous because entropy increases.
explanation.final_answer: B. Because it generally increases entropy
explanation.simple_explanation: Gases mix naturally because the mixed state is more disordered. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: An increase in disorder of a system corresponds to

explanation.question_restate: We are to relate disorder to entropy.
explanation.principle: Entropy is a measure of disorder or randomness.
steps: When particles become more spread out or less ordered, randomness increases.
That means the entropy also increases.
So increasing disorder corresponds directly to increasing entropy.
Therefore the answer is increase in entropy.
explanation.simple_explanation: More disorder means higher entropy. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Enthalpy changes, (ΔH), in physical and chemical processes
question: If heat is released to the surroundings during a reaction, the reaction is

explanation.question_restate: We are to name a reaction that gives out heat.
explanation.principle: Heat release to the surroundings is the defining feature of an exothermic reaction.
steps: In such a reaction, the system loses heat energy.
That is why the surroundings may become warmer.
The enthalpy change for this type of reaction is negative.
Therefore the reaction is exothermic.
explanation.simple_explanation: If heat comes out, the reaction is exothermic. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Which of the following commonly causes cooling when dissolved in water?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify a substance whose dissolution is commonly cooling.
explanation.principle: A cooling effect means the process absorbs heat from the surroundings.
steps: Ammonium chloride is a familiar example of an endothermic dissolution.
NaOH and KOH dissolve exothermically and usually warm the solution.
Concentrated sulfuric acid mixing with water also releases a lot of heat.
Therefore NH₄Cl is the correct choice.
explanation.simple_explanation: Ammonium chloride cools water because its dissolution is endothermic. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: At equilibrium in this topic, ΔG° is

explanation.question_restate: We are to recall the Gibbs free energy condition for equilibrium in this syllabus.
explanation.principle: At equilibrium, the net tendency for change is balanced.
steps: The criterion given here is that ΔG° = 0 at equilibrium.
This means the system has no net spontaneous push in either direction.
It is a key condition to remember for exam purposes.
Therefore ΔG° is zero at equilibrium.
explanation.simple_explanation: At equilibrium, ΔG° is zero. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

explanation.question_restate: We are to relate disorder to entropy.
explanation.principle: Entropy is a measure of disorder or randomness.
steps: When particles become more spread out or less ordered, randomness increases.
That means the entropy also increases.
So increasing disorder corresponds directly to increasing entropy.
Therefore the answer is increase in entropy.
explanation.simple_explanation: More disorder means higher entropy. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

Concentrated sulfuric acid mixing with water also releases a lot of heat.
Therefore NH₄Cl is the correct choice.
explanation.simple_explanation: Ammonium chloride cools water because its dissolution is endothermic. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: At equilibrium in this topic, ΔG° is

explanation.question_restate: We are to recall the Gibbs free energy condition for equilibrium in this syllabus.
explanation.principle: At equilibrium, the net tendency for change is balanced.
steps: The criterion given here is that ΔG° = 0 at equilibrium.
This means the system has no net spontaneous push in either direction.
It is a key condition to remember for exam purposes.
Therefore ΔG° is zero at equilibrium.
explanation.simple_explanation: At equilibrium, ΔG° is zero. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: An increase in disorder of a system corresponds to

explanation.question_restate: We are to relate disorder to entropy.
explanation.principle: Entropy is a measure of disorder or randomness.
steps: When particles become more spread out or less ordered, randomness increases.
That means the entropy also increases.
So increasing disorder corresponds directly to increasing entropy.
Therefore the answer is increase in entropy.
explanation.simple_explanation: More disorder means higher entropy. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Enthalpy changes, (ΔH), in physical and chemical processes
question: Which of the following statements about enthalpy change is correct?

options.A: ΔH measures the speed of a reaction
options.B: ΔH measures the heat change at constant pressure
options.C: ΔH is the same as activation energy

answer_text: ΔH measures the heat change at constant pressure

explanation.question_restate: We are to choose the correct meaning of enthalpy change.
explanation.principle: Enthalpy change, ΔH, is used to describe the heat absorbed or released when a process occurs at constant pressure.
steps: If heat is released, ΔH is negative.
If heat is absorbed, ΔH is positive.
This quantity does not tell us how fast the reaction is.
Therefore ΔH measures the heat change at constant pressure.
explanation.final_answer: B. ΔH measures the heat change at constant pressure
explanation.simple_explanation: Enthalpy change tells us whether heat is taken in or given out at constant pressure. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Enthalpy changes, (ΔH), in physical and chemical processes
question: If the products of a reaction have lower enthalpy than the reactants, the reaction is

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the type of reaction when product enthalpy is lower than reactant enthalpy.
explanation.principle: A drop in enthalpy means the system has lost heat to the surroundings.
steps: If the products are at a lower enthalpy level, energy must have been released during the change.
Release of heat means the reaction is exothermic.
In such a case, ΔH is negative.
Therefore the reaction is exothermic.
explanation.simple_explanation: When products are lower in enthalpy than reactants, heat has been released. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Which observation most suggests that a process is exothermic?

options.B: The temperature of the surroundings rises
options.C: Ice forms in the solution immediately

answer_text: The temperature of the surroundings rises

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify a sign that a process gives out heat.
explanation.principle: Exothermic processes release heat to the surroundings.
steps: When heat is released, the nearby surroundings become warmer.
That may be noticed as a rise in the temperature of the container or solution.
A drop in surrounding temperature would instead suggest an endothermic process.
Therefore a rise in the temperature of the surroundings suggests an exothermic process.
explanation.final_answer: B. The temperature of the surroundings rises
explanation.simple_explanation: Exothermic processes warm their surroundings because they release heat. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: A reaction mixture becomes colder during a chemical change. This suggests that the reaction is

explanation.question_restate: We are to interpret a drop in temperature during a reaction.
explanation.principle: If the mixture becomes colder, heat is being absorbed from the surroundings.
steps: An endothermic process takes in heat.
As the reaction absorbs heat, the solution or surroundings may cool down.
This cooling effect is a practical sign of an endothermic change.
Therefore the reaction is endothermic.
explanation.simple_explanation: If a process cools its surroundings, it is taking in heat and is endothermic. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: When potassium reacts with water, the solution formed is alkaline because it contains

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the substance responsible for the alkaline solution formed when potassium reacts with water.
explanation.principle: Potassium reacts with water to produce potassium hydroxide and hydrogen gas.
steps: The reaction is 2K + 2H₂O → 2KOH + H₂.
Potassium hydroxide is a strong alkali.
Its presence makes the resulting solution alkaline.
Therefore the alkaline substance formed is KOH.
explanation.simple_explanation: Potassium forms potassium hydroxide in water, and that makes the solution alkaline. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Which of the following reactions with water is most likely to be the most vigorous?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the most vigorous process among the options.
explanation.principle: Potassium is a very reactive Group I metal and reacts strongly with water.
steps: NaOH dissolving is exothermic but is not usually as violent as potassium metal reacting.
NH₄Cl dissolving is endothermic and gentle.
Melting of ice is a physical process and not vigorous.
Therefore potassium reacting with water is the most vigorous.
explanation.simple_explanation: Potassium reacts very strongly with water because it is a highly reactive alkali metal. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: The dissolution of NaOH in water is a common example of a

explanation.question_restate: We are to classify the dissolution of sodium hydroxide in water.
explanation.principle: Sodium hydroxide dissolving in water releases a noticeable amount of heat.
steps: As NaOH dissolves, strong interactions form between its ions and water molecules.
These interactions release energy.
That is why the solution becomes warm.
Therefore the dissolution is strongly exothermic.
explanation.simple_explanation: Sodium hydroxide warms the solution when it dissolves, so the process is exothermic. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Which of the following salt dissolutions is commonly used in cold packs because it absorbs heat?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify a salt whose dissolution absorbs heat from the surroundings.
explanation.principle: A salt used in a cold pack must dissolve endothermically.
steps: Ammonium chloride is a standard example of a salt that cools the solution when it dissolves.
NaOH, CaCl₂ and KOH typically release heat on dissolving.
Because NH₄Cl absorbs heat, it can create a cooling effect.
Therefore NH₄Cl is the correct choice.
explanation.simple_explanation: Cold packs use salts like NH₄Cl because they absorb heat as they dissolve. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Which change is accompanied by an increase in entropy?

options.D: gas being compressed into a smaller volume

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify a change that increases disorder.
explanation.principle: Entropy increases when matter becomes less ordered and particles gain more freedom of movement.
steps: A solid has particles in fixed, ordered positions.
A liquid has particles that are less ordered and can move around more freely.
So melting from solid to liquid increases randomness.
Therefore solid melting to liquid is accompanied by an increase in entropy.
explanation.simple_explanation: Melting makes particles less ordered, so entropy increases. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Why does a gas generally have higher entropy than a solid?

options.B: Because gas particles are more widely separated and more randomly arranged

answer_text: Because gas particles are more widely separated and more randomly arranged

explanation.question_restate: We are to explain why gases have greater entropy than solids.
explanation.principle: Entropy is linked with the degree of randomness and the number of possible arrangements of particles.
steps: In solids, particles are close together and occupy fairly fixed positions.
In gases, particles move freely and can be arranged in many more ways.
That greater freedom gives a much higher degree of disorder.
Therefore gases have higher entropy because their particles are more widely separated and randomly arranged.
explanation.final_answer: B. Because gas particles are more widely separated and more randomly arranged
explanation.simple_explanation: Gas particles have much more freedom of movement, so the gas state is more disordered. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Two gases initially separated in a container are allowed to mix. The main reason this mixing is favored is that

options.B: the entropy of the system increases

answer_text: the entropy of the system increases

explanation.question_restate: We are to state the main thermodynamic reason gases mix spontaneously.
explanation.principle: Mixing gives a more disordered arrangement of particles than keeping the gases separated.
steps: Before mixing, each gas occupies its own region.
After mixing, both gases are spread randomly throughout the available space.
This increase in randomness means an increase in entropy.
Therefore the main reason the mixing is favored is that entropy increases.
explanation.final_answer: B. the entropy of the system increases
explanation.simple_explanation: Gases mix naturally because the mixed state is more disordered. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Which statement about spontaneity is correct?

options.A: A spontaneous process must always be fast
options.B: A spontaneous process is thermodynamically favorable under the given conditions
options.C: A spontaneous process must be exothermic
options.D: A spontaneous process never needs activation energy

answer_text: A spontaneous process is thermodynamically favorable under the given conditions

explanation.question_restate: We are to choose the correct meaning of spontaneity.
explanation.principle: Spontaneity refers to whether a process is naturally favored, not how quickly it happens.
steps: A reaction can be spontaneous and still slow if the activation energy is high.
A spontaneous process does not have to be exothermic in every case.
The key idea is that it is thermodynamically favorable under the conditions considered.
Therefore that is the correct statement.
explanation.final_answer: B. A spontaneous process is thermodynamically favorable under the given conditions
explanation.simple_explanation: Spontaneous means favorable by thermodynamics, not necessarily fast. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: A reaction with ΔG° > 0 under standard conditions is

explanation.question_restate: We are to classify a reaction using the sign of ΔG°.
explanation.principle: A positive Gibbs free energy means the process is not thermodynamically favorable under the given conditions.
steps: If ΔG° is greater than zero, the reaction does not proceed spontaneously in the forward direction under standard conditions.
Energy input or a change in conditions would be needed to drive it.
This is the opposite of the spontaneous case where ΔG° is negative.
Therefore the process is non-spontaneous.
explanation.simple_explanation: Positive ΔG° means the forward process is not spontaneous. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: A reaction with ΔG° < 0 is expected to be

explanation.question_restate: We are to determine what a negative standard Gibbs free energy implies.
explanation.principle: A negative ΔG° indicates that the process is thermodynamically favorable under standard conditions.
steps: Such a reaction has a natural tendency to proceed in the forward direction.
This is the standard criterion for spontaneity in Gibbs free energy analysis.
It does not necessarily mean the reaction is fast, only that it is favorable.
Therefore a reaction with ΔG° < 0 is spontaneous.
explanation.simple_explanation: Negative ΔG° is the sign for a spontaneous change. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: If ΔG° = 0, the system is said to be

explanation.question_restate: We are to state the condition represented by ΔG° = 0 in this topic.
explanation.principle: At equilibrium, there is no net thermodynamic tendency for change in either direction.
steps: The syllabus condition given here is ΔG° = 0 at equilibrium.
This means forward and reverse tendencies balance each other.
There is no net spontaneous driving force left.
Therefore the system is at equilibrium.
explanation.simple_explanation: When ΔG° is zero, the system is at equilibrium. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Using ΔG = ΔH − TΔS, a process is more likely to become spontaneous at high temperature if it has

options.C: ΔH negative and ΔS negative only

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the sign combination that can make a process become spontaneous at higher temperature.
explanation.principle: As temperature rises, the size of the TΔS term becomes more important in the Gibbs equation.
steps: If ΔS is positive, then −TΔS becomes more negative as temperature increases.
This can help overcome a positive ΔH.
So an endothermic process may still become spontaneous if the entropy increase is large enough at high temperature.
Therefore ΔH positive and ΔS positive is the correct combination.
explanation.final_answer: A. ΔH positive and ΔS positive
explanation.simple_explanation: A positive entropy change helps high temperature make ΔG negative. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: If ΔH = −50 kJ mol⁻¹ and TΔS = −20 kJ mol⁻¹, then ΔG is

explanation.question_restate: We are to calculate ΔG from the Gibbs equation.
explanation.principle: Use ΔG = ΔH − TΔS carefully, paying attention to the signs.
steps: Substitute the given values: ΔG = −50 − (−20).
Subtracting a negative is the same as adding a positive.
So ΔG = −50 + 20 = −30 kJ mol⁻¹.
Therefore the correct value is −30 kJ mol⁻¹.
explanation.simple_explanation: Be careful with signs: minus a negative becomes plus. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Dissolution of many salts in water is often accompanied by an increase in entropy because

options.A: the ions become more dispersed in solution
options.B: the ions become fixed in a lattice
options.C: the salt always freezes the water
options.D: the mass of the solution decreases

answer_text: the ions become more dispersed in solution

explanation.question_restate: We are to explain why entropy often increases when a salt dissolves.
explanation.principle: When a crystal dissolves, its particles become more spread out in the solvent.
steps: In the solid crystal, the ions are fixed in an ordered lattice.
When the salt dissolves, the ions separate and spread through the water.
This creates a more disordered arrangement than before.
Therefore entropy often increases because the ions become more dispersed in solution.
explanation.final_answer: A. the ions become more dispersed in solution
explanation.simple_explanation: Dissolving breaks up the ordered lattice and spreads the ions out, increasing disorder. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: A reaction profile showing products below reactants indicates a reaction that is

explanation.question_restate: We are to interpret an energy profile in which the products lie below the reactants.
explanation.principle: Lower product enthalpy means energy has been released during the reaction.
steps: If the products are at a lower enthalpy level, the system has lost energy to the surroundings.
That means the reaction is exothermic.
For an exothermic reaction, ΔH is negative.
Therefore the reaction is exothermic with negative ΔH.
explanation.simple_explanation: Products lower than reactants means energy was released, so ΔH is negative. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Which substance among the following is most commonly used as an example of an endothermic dissolution in water?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the substance commonly used to illustrate endothermic dissolution.
explanation.principle: An endothermic dissolution absorbs heat and cools the surroundings.
steps: Ammonium chloride is a standard school example of a salt that dissolves endothermically.
NaOH and KOH usually dissolve exothermically.
Sodium metal reacts chemically with water rather than simply dissolving.
Therefore NH₄Cl is the correct example.
explanation.simple_explanation: NH₄Cl is a common classroom example of a salt that cools water as it dissolves. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: When disorder in a system decreases, entropy

explanation.question_restate: We are to relate decreasing disorder to entropy.
explanation.principle: Entropy measures the degree of disorder or randomness of a system.
steps: If a system becomes more ordered, its disorder decreases.
That means the entropy becomes smaller.
So a decrease in disorder corresponds to a decrease in entropy.
Therefore entropy decreases.
explanation.simple_explanation: More order means lower entropy. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: A process that can occur on its own under the stated conditions is described as

explanation.question_restate: We are to choose the term for a process that can occur naturally under the given conditions.
explanation.principle: The word used in thermodynamics for such a favorable process is spontaneous.
steps: A spontaneous process does not require continuous outside driving force.
It is naturally favored by the conditions.
This says nothing by itself about how fast it happens.
Therefore the correct term is spontaneous.
explanation.simple_explanation: A process that can go on by itself under the conditions is called spontaneous. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: A negative value of ΔG° indicates that the change is

explanation.question_restate: We are to interpret the meaning of a negative ΔG°.
explanation.principle: Negative Gibbs free energy means the process is thermodynamically favorable.
steps: This is the standard criterion for spontaneity in Gibbs free energy analysis.
It does not tell us the rate directly, only the favorability.
So if ΔG° is less than zero, the change is spontaneous.
Therefore the correct answer is spontaneous.
explanation.simple_explanation: Negative ΔG° means the process is thermodynamically favored. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: A reaction has ΔH = −120 kJ mol⁻¹. This means the reaction

explanation.question_restate: We are to interpret a negative enthalpy change numerically.
explanation.principle: A negative ΔH means the process is exothermic and heat is released to the surroundings.
steps: The minus sign tells us that the system loses enthalpy.
Losing enthalpy means heat leaves the reacting system.
The magnitude, 120 kJ mol⁻¹, tells us how much heat is released per mole of reaction.
Therefore the reaction releases 120 kJ mol⁻¹ of heat.
explanation.final_answer: B. releases 120 kJ mol⁻¹ of heat
explanation.simple_explanation: A negative ΔH means heat comes out of the system. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Enthalpy changes, (ΔH), in physical and chemical processes
question: If the enthalpy change for a process is +35 kJ mol⁻¹, the process is

explanation.question_restate: We are to classify a process from the sign of its enthalpy change.
explanation.principle: A positive ΔH means the system absorbs heat from the surroundings.
steps: When the value of ΔH is greater than zero, heat is taken in.
Processes that absorb heat are called endothermic.
So a ΔH of +35 kJ mol⁻¹ indicates an endothermic process.
Therefore the correct answer is endothermic.
explanation.simple_explanation: Positive ΔH means the process absorbs heat, so it is endothermic. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Which of the following substances gives an exothermic dissolution in water?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify a substance that releases heat on dissolving in water.
explanation.principle: An exothermic dissolution warms the solution because heat is released.
steps: Ammonium chloride is a well-known endothermic example.
Sodium hydroxide dissolves and the solution becomes warm.
That means heat is released during the dissolution of NaOH.
Therefore NaOH gives an exothermic dissolution.
explanation.simple_explanation: NaOH warms the water when it dissolves, so the dissolution is exothermic. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: When sodium reacts with water, one reason the reaction can appear violent is that

options.B: heat released can ignite the hydrogen produced

answer_text: heat released can ignite the hydrogen produced

explanation.question_restate: We are to explain why sodium reacting with water can appear violent.
explanation.principle: The reaction is strongly exothermic and also produces hydrogen gas.
steps: Sodium reacts with water to form sodium hydroxide and hydrogen gas.
The reaction releases heat.
That heat can sometimes ignite the hydrogen produced, making the reaction seem violent.
Therefore the heat released can ignite the hydrogen.
explanation.final_answer: B. heat released can ignite the hydrogen produced
explanation.simple_explanation: The reaction gives off heat and hydrogen gas, and the hydrogen may catch fire. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Which of the following processes is most likely to lower the temperature of its surroundings?

options.B: neutralization of an acid by a base

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the process that cools its surroundings.
explanation.principle: A process lowers surrounding temperature when it absorbs heat from them.
steps: Combustion and neutralization are usually exothermic.
Burning magnesium also releases a lot of heat.
Ammonium chloride dissolving in water is endothermic, so it takes in heat.
Therefore dissolution of NH₄Cl lowers the temperature of its surroundings.
explanation.simple_explanation: A process that absorbs heat cools the surroundings, and NH₄Cl is a standard example. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Which process is accompanied by a decrease in entropy?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the process that makes a system more ordered.
explanation.principle: Entropy decreases when the arrangement of particles becomes more ordered.
steps: In freezing, liquid water becomes solid ice.
The particles become arranged in a more fixed and ordered structure.
That means the degree of disorder decreases.
Therefore freezing of water is accompanied by a decrease in entropy.
explanation.simple_explanation: When liquid turns to solid, the particles become more ordered, so entropy decreases. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Which of the following changes would most likely produce the greatest increase in entropy?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the change that produces the biggest rise in disorder.
explanation.principle: Entropy increases most when particles gain the greatest freedom of motion and arrangement.
steps: A liquid already has some freedom of movement.
A gas has much greater freedom and much larger separation between particles.
So changing from liquid to gas causes a large increase in disorder.
Therefore liquid → gas gives the greatest increase in entropy among the options.
explanation.simple_explanation: The liquid-to-gas change gives particles the greatest freedom, so entropy rises most. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: The spontaneous mixing of gases can be explained mainly by the fact that the mixed state

explanation.question_restate: We are to explain why the mixed state of gases is favored.
explanation.principle: The mixed state is more random than the separated state.
steps: When gases are separate, each occupies its own part of the container.
After mixing, the particles are distributed more randomly throughout the whole volume.
This greater randomness means the mixed state has higher entropy.
Therefore the mixing is favored because the mixed state has higher entropy.
explanation.simple_explanation: The mixed state is more disordered, so its entropy is higher. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: A spontaneous process under given conditions is one that

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the correct meaning of spontaneity.
explanation.principle: Spontaneous means thermodynamically favored under the stated conditions.
steps: A spontaneous process may still be very slow if it has a high activation energy.
It also does not have to be exothermic in every case.
What matters is whether the process is favored by thermodynamics.
Therefore a spontaneous process is thermodynamically favorable.
explanation.simple_explanation: Spontaneous means the process is naturally favored, not necessarily fast. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: At equilibrium, the value of ΔG° is

explanation.question_restate: We are to state the value of the standard Gibbs free energy change at equilibrium.
explanation.principle: At equilibrium there is no net driving force for change in one direction.
steps: The syllabus relation for this topic states that ΔG° = 0 at equilibrium.
This means the forward and reverse tendencies are balanced.
So the system has no net thermodynamic push in either direction.
Therefore ΔG° is zero.
explanation.simple_explanation: At equilibrium, ΔG° is zero. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: If ΔH = +20 kJ mol⁻¹ and TΔS = +50 kJ mol⁻¹, the value of ΔG is

explanation.question_restate: We are to calculate ΔG using the Gibbs free energy equation.
explanation.principle: Use the relation ΔG = ΔH − TΔS.
steps: Substitute the given values: ΔG = +20 − 50.
That gives −30 kJ mol⁻¹.
A negative result means the process is thermodynamically favorable.
Therefore the value of ΔG is −30 kJ mol⁻¹.
explanation.simple_explanation: Subtract TΔS from ΔH to get ΔG. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: If ΔG° is positive, the forward reaction under standard conditions is

explanation.question_restate: We are to interpret the meaning of a positive standard Gibbs free energy change.
explanation.principle: A positive ΔG° means the process is not favored in the forward direction under standard conditions.
steps: When ΔG° is greater than zero, the reaction does not proceed spontaneously forward under standard conditions.
This is the thermodynamic criterion for non-spontaneity.
The reverse process would be more favorable.
Therefore the forward reaction is non-spontaneous.
explanation.simple_explanation: Positive ΔG° means the forward change is not thermodynamically favored. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: A negative value of ΔG° shows that a process is

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify what is implied by a negative standard Gibbs free energy.
explanation.principle: Negative ΔG° indicates that the process is thermodynamically favorable under standard conditions.
steps: This is the key criterion for spontaneity in Gibbs free energy analysis.
It does not guarantee a fast rate, only favorability.
So a negative ΔG° means the process can occur spontaneously.
Therefore the process is spontaneous.
explanation.simple_explanation: Negative ΔG° is the sign for a spontaneous change. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Which of the following statements correctly distinguishes spontaneity from rate?

options.A: Spontaneous reactions must always be fast
options.B: Rate tells whether a reaction is favorable, while spontaneity tells how fast it is
options.C: Spontaneity tells whether a process is favored, while rate tells how fast it happens
options.D: They mean exactly the same thing

answer_text: Spontaneity tells whether a process is favored, while rate tells how fast it happens

explanation.question_restate: We are to distinguish between spontaneity and reaction rate.
explanation.principle: Spontaneity is a thermodynamic idea, while rate is a kinetic idea.
steps: A reaction may be spontaneous but still occur slowly if the activation energy is high.
Rate describes how quickly the reaction proceeds.
Spontaneity describes whether the change is thermodynamically favorable.
Therefore the correct distinction is that spontaneity tells whether a process is favored, while rate tells how fast it happens.
explanation.final_answer: C. Spontaneity tells whether a process is favored, while rate tells how fast it happens
explanation.simple_explanation: Thermodynamics tells whether a change is favored; kinetics tells how fast it occurs. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: When a crystalline solid dissolves to form freely moving ions in water, the entropy generally

explanation.question_restate: We are to state what happens to entropy when a crystalline ionic solid dissolves in water.
explanation.principle: Dissolving usually spreads the particles out and makes the arrangement more random.
steps: A crystal lattice is highly ordered.
When it dissolves, the ions move apart and become distributed through the solution.
This greater dispersion increases disorder.
Therefore the entropy generally increases.
explanation.simple_explanation: Breaking up an ordered crystal into freely moving ions usually increases entropy. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Which of the following best explains why gases have high entropy?

options.A: Their particles are fixed in position
options.B: Their particles are close-packed and ordered
options.C: Their particles move freely and can occupy many arrangements

answer_text: Their particles move freely and can occupy many arrangements

explanation.question_restate: We are to explain why gases have high entropy.
explanation.principle: Entropy is larger when particles can be arranged in many possible ways.
steps: Gas particles move freely and are widely separated.
Because of that, there are many possible positions and arrangements available to them.
This gives gases a high degree of randomness.
Therefore gases have high entropy because their particles move freely and can occupy many arrangements.
explanation.final_answer: C. Their particles move freely and can occupy many arrangements
explanation.simple_explanation: More possible arrangements mean greater disorder and therefore higher entropy. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Which of the following is a chemical reaction with water rather than simple dissolution?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify which process is an actual chemical reaction with water.
explanation.principle: Some substances simply dissolve, while others react chemically with water to form new substances.
steps: Sugar and many salts mainly dissolve without forming entirely new products.
Sodium metal reacts with water to produce sodium hydroxide and hydrogen gas.
Because new substances are formed, this is a chemical reaction.
Therefore Na in water is the correct answer.
explanation.simple_explanation: Sodium does not just dissolve; it reacts chemically with water. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: The strong heating effect observed when NaOH is added to water is due to

options.D: the formation of a gas only

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the reason for the warming effect when sodium hydroxide is added to water.
explanation.principle: If a process makes the solution warmer, heat is being released.
steps: Sodium hydroxide dissolves and hydrates strongly in water.
The process releases heat overall.
That is why the container or solution becomes warm.
Therefore the heating effect is due to an exothermic dissolution.
explanation.simple_explanation: NaOH dissolves with heat release, so the solution becomes warm. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Enthalpy changes, (ΔH), in physical and chemical processes
question: A reaction in which the products are higher in enthalpy than the reactants is

explanation.question_restate: We are to interpret the relative enthalpy levels of reactants and products.
explanation.principle: If products are higher in enthalpy, the system must have absorbed heat to reach that level.
steps: Higher product enthalpy means the final state contains more heat content than the initial state.
That extra energy must have been absorbed from the surroundings.
Absorption of heat means the reaction is endothermic.
Therefore the reaction is endothermic.
explanation.simple_explanation: If products end up higher in enthalpy, heat was absorbed, so the process is endothermic. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: A process has ΔH = +10 kJ mol⁻¹ and TΔS = +5 kJ mol⁻¹. The sign of ΔG is

explanation.question_restate: We are to determine the sign of ΔG from given ΔH and TΔS values.
explanation.principle: Use the equation ΔG = ΔH − TΔS.
steps: Substitute the values: ΔG = +10 − 5.
This gives +5 kJ mol⁻¹.
Because the result is positive, the sign of ΔG is positive.
Therefore the correct answer is positive.
explanation.simple_explanation: Subtract TΔS from ΔH; here the result stays positive. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: An increase in randomness of particles is associated with

explanation.question_restate: We are to relate randomness to entropy.
explanation.principle: Entropy is a measure of disorder or randomness.
steps: If particles become more randomly arranged, disorder increases.
An increase in disorder means an increase in entropy.
So more randomness corresponds to higher entropy.
Therefore the entropy increases.
explanation.simple_explanation: More disorder means higher entropy. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: A process that occurs naturally under the given conditions is described as

explanation.question_restate: We are to choose the term for a process that can occur naturally under the given conditions.
explanation.principle: The thermodynamic word used for such a process is spontaneous.
steps: A spontaneous change does not require continuous outside assistance to proceed.
This does not mean the process must be fast.
It only means the change is naturally favored.
Therefore the process is described as spontaneous.
explanation.simple_explanation: A naturally favored process is called spontaneous. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: For a spontaneous process under standard conditions, ΔG° is

explanation.question_restate: We are to state the sign of ΔG° for a spontaneous process.
explanation.principle: A spontaneous process has negative standard Gibbs free energy.
steps: Negative ΔG° means the process is thermodynamically favorable.
Positive ΔG° would mean non-spontaneous.
Zero corresponds to equilibrium in this topic.
Therefore ΔG° is less than zero for a spontaneous process.
explanation.simple_explanation: Spontaneous change means negative ΔG°. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

explanation.simple_explanation: A naturally favored process is called spontaneous. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

explanation.principle: A spontaneous process has negative standard Gibbs free energy.

Therefore ΔG° is less than zero for a spontaneous process.

subtopic: Enthalpy changes, (ΔH), in physical and chemical processes
question: In an exothermic energy profile, the products are usually shown

options.C: at the same level as the reactants always

explanation.question_restate: We are to interpret how products are placed relative to reactants on an exothermic energy profile.
explanation.principle: In an exothermic process, the products have lower enthalpy than the reactants.
steps: An exothermic reaction releases heat to the surroundings.
Because energy is released, the final enthalpy is lower than the initial enthalpy.
On an energy profile, this is shown by placing the products below the reactants.
Therefore the products are usually shown below the reactants.
explanation.simple_explanation: Exothermic reactions end at a lower enthalpy level than they started. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Enthalpy changes, (ΔH), in physical and chemical processes
question: A positive value of ΔH for a process means that the products have

options.C: the same enthalpy as the reactants

explanation.question_restate: We are to compare product and reactant enthalpy when ΔH is positive.
explanation.principle: A positive ΔH shows that the process absorbs heat and the system finishes at a higher enthalpy level.
steps: Endothermic processes have positive ΔH.
When heat is absorbed, the final enthalpy becomes greater than the initial enthalpy.
So the products are higher in enthalpy than the reactants.
Therefore the correct choice is that the products have higher enthalpy.
explanation.final_answer: B. higher enthalpy than the reactants
explanation.simple_explanation: Positive ΔH means the system ends up at a higher enthalpy level. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Which of the following processes is mainly physical rather than chemical?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify a process that is mainly physical rather than a full chemical reaction forming new substances.
explanation.principle: Dissolution often involves separating particles and hydrating them, whereas chemical reactions form new substances.
steps: When sodium or potassium reacts with water, new substances are formed, including hydroxides and hydrogen gas.
Combustion also forms entirely new products.
Dissolving NaOH mainly involves the solid separating into ions and mixing with water.
So NaOH dissolving in water is the mainly physical process here.
explanation.simple_explanation: Dissolution is usually treated as a physical change, while reactions with water produce new substances. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: The reaction of sodium with water can be represented by

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the correct equation for sodium reacting with water.
explanation.principle: Group I metals react with water to form the metal hydroxide and hydrogen gas.
steps: Sodium reacts with water to give sodium hydroxide in solution and hydrogen gas.
The balanced equation is 2Na + 2H₂O → 2NaOH + H₂.
This shows equal numbers of atoms of each element on both sides.
Therefore option A is the correct equation.
explanation.simple_explanation: Sodium and water produce sodium hydroxide and hydrogen. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: A process that absorbs heat from the surroundings will usually make the surroundings

explanation.question_restate: We are to determine the effect on the surroundings when heat is absorbed.
explanation.principle: An endothermic process takes heat from its surroundings.
steps: If the system absorbs heat, that heat comes from nearby surroundings.
As the surroundings lose heat, their temperature tends to drop.
This is why endothermic processes often feel cold.
Therefore the surroundings become cooler.
explanation.simple_explanation: When heat is absorbed by the process, the surroundings lose heat and cool down. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Which of the following changes leads to a more ordered system?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify a change that makes matter more ordered.
explanation.principle: Order increases when particles become closer together and have less freedom of motion.
steps: Melting and boiling increase freedom of movement and increase disorder.
Mixing gases also increases randomness.
Condensation changes gas into liquid, bringing particles closer together and reducing randomness.
Therefore steam condensing to liquid leads to a more ordered system.
explanation.simple_explanation: Condensation makes particles less free and more ordered. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Which of the following arrangements has the lowest entropy?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the arrangement with the least disorder.
explanation.principle: Lower entropy means a more ordered state with fewer possible particle arrangements.
steps: Gas particles are highly disordered and therefore have high entropy.
Aqueous solutions are more disordered than pure crystals because particles are dispersed.
Ice crystals have water molecules arranged in a relatively fixed, ordered lattice.
Therefore ice crystals have the lowest entropy among the options.
explanation.simple_explanation: A crystal lattice is highly ordered, so it has low entropy. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: When hydrogen and oxygen are allowed to mix without reacting, the entropy of the mixture

explanation.question_restate: We are to determine what happens to entropy when two gases mix physically.
explanation.principle: Mixing gases spreads the particles more randomly through the available space.
steps: Before mixing, each gas occupies its own region.
After mixing, the molecules are distributed more randomly.
This greater disorder means higher entropy.
Therefore the entropy of the mixture increases.
explanation.simple_explanation: Mixing gases increases randomness, so entropy rises. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: A reaction may be spontaneous but slow because

options.B: its activation energy may be high

answer_text: its activation energy may be high

explanation.question_restate: We are to explain how a spontaneous reaction can still proceed slowly.
explanation.principle: Spontaneity is a thermodynamic idea, while reaction rate depends on activation energy and kinetics.
steps: A reaction can be thermodynamically favorable and still face a large energy barrier.
That barrier is called the activation energy.
If the activation energy is high, the reaction can be slow even though it is spontaneous.
Therefore the reason is that the activation energy may be high.
explanation.final_answer: B. its activation energy may be high
explanation.simple_explanation: A reaction can be favorable overall but still slow if it has a large activation energy barrier. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: At a given temperature, a process will be spontaneous if

explanation.question_restate: We are to state the Gibbs free energy condition for spontaneity.
explanation.principle: A negative Gibbs free energy means the process is thermodynamically favorable.
steps: The sign of ΔG tells us whether a process can occur spontaneously under the conditions.
If ΔG is less than zero, the process is favored.
If ΔG is greater than zero, it is non-spontaneous.
Therefore a process is spontaneous when ΔG < 0.
explanation.simple_explanation: Negative Gibbs free energy means a spontaneous process. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: If ΔH is negative and ΔS is positive, the reaction is usually

explanation.question_restate: We are to determine the likely spontaneity when ΔH is negative and ΔS is positive.
explanation.principle: Use the Gibbs equation ΔG = ΔH − TΔS.
steps: A negative ΔH favors spontaneity.
A positive ΔS also favors spontaneity because the term −TΔS becomes negative.
Since both terms help make ΔG negative, the process is usually spontaneous at all temperatures.
Therefore the correct answer is spontaneous at all temperatures.
explanation.simple_explanation: Negative ΔH and positive ΔS both favor a negative ΔG. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: If ΔH is positive and ΔS is negative, the reaction is usually

explanation.question_restate: We are to infer spontaneity from the signs of ΔH and ΔS.
explanation.principle: Use the Gibbs equation ΔG = ΔH − TΔS.
steps: A positive ΔH works against spontaneity.
A negative ΔS means the term −TΔS becomes positive, which also works against spontaneity.
Since both effects make ΔG positive, the process is usually non-spontaneous at all temperatures.
Therefore the correct answer is non-spontaneous at all temperatures.
explanation.simple_explanation: Positive ΔH and negative ΔS both oppose spontaneity, so ΔG stays positive. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: For a process to be at equilibrium, the appropriate condition in this topic is

explanation.question_restate: We are to state the Gibbs free energy condition for equilibrium in this syllabus.
explanation.principle: At equilibrium, the system has no net thermodynamic driving force in one direction.
steps: The topic states that ΔG° = 0 at equilibrium.
This means the forward and reverse tendencies balance one another.
So there is no net spontaneous change overall.
Therefore the correct condition is ΔG° = 0.
explanation.simple_explanation: At equilibrium, the Gibbs free energy change is zero. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Why does dissolving a crystal in water often increase disorder?

options.A: Because the ions or molecules become more spread out in solution

answer_text: Because the ions or molecules become more spread out in solution

explanation.question_restate: We are to explain why entropy often rises when a crystal dissolves.
explanation.principle: A crystal is an ordered arrangement, while a solution often has particles more randomly distributed.
steps: In the solid crystal, particles occupy fixed positions in a lattice.
When the crystal dissolves, those particles move into the water and become dispersed.
This greater dispersion increases randomness.
Therefore entropy often increases because the particles become more spread out.
explanation.final_answer: A. Because the ions or molecules become more spread out in solution
explanation.simple_explanation: A dissolved substance is usually more disordered than a crystal lattice. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Two gases initially in separate halves of a container are allowed to mix. The entropy change is usually

explanation.question_restate: We are to determine the sign of the entropy change for gas mixing.
explanation.principle: Mixing increases randomness because particles become distributed through a larger accessible region.
steps: Initially, each gas is confined to part of the container.
After mixing, both gases are spread more randomly throughout the entire volume.
This increase in disorder means the entropy change is positive.
Therefore the entropy change is usually positive.
explanation.simple_explanation: Gas mixing gives a more random arrangement, so ΔS is positive. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: The process NaOH(s) → Na⁺(aq) + OH⁻(aq) is best described as

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the kind of process shown when solid NaOH becomes aqueous ions.
explanation.principle: This change describes a solid separating into ions in water.
steps: No gas is produced in the equation given.
Instead, the solid enters solution and becomes hydrated ions.
That is the meaning of dissolution in water.
Therefore the process is dissolution.
explanation.simple_explanation: NaOH is dissolving to form aqueous ions. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: When potassium reacts with water, the gas evolved is

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the gas released when potassium reacts with water.
explanation.principle: Group I metals react with water to form the metal hydroxide and hydrogen gas.
steps: The reaction produces potassium hydroxide and hydrogen.
Hydrogen is the gaseous product in the reaction.
This is a standard feature of alkali metals reacting with water.
Therefore the gas evolved is H₂.
explanation.simple_explanation: Potassium and water produce hydrogen gas. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: Combustion reactions are generally

explanation.question_restate: We are to classify combustion reactions in terms of heat flow.
explanation.principle: Combustion usually releases a lot of heat to the surroundings.
steps: When a substance burns, chemical energy is converted into heat and often light.
This heat release is the hallmark of an exothermic process.
That is why flames and warming are observed in combustion.
Therefore combustion reactions are generally exothermic.
explanation.simple_explanation: Burning releases heat, so combustion is exothermic. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: If ΔH = −10 kJ mol⁻¹ and TΔS = +20 kJ mol⁻¹, then ΔG is

explanation.question_restate: We are to calculate ΔG from the given values.
explanation.principle: Use the equation ΔG = ΔH − TΔS.
steps: Substitute the values directly: ΔG = −10 − 20.
This gives −30 kJ mol⁻¹.
Because the result is negative, the process is thermodynamically favorable.
Therefore the correct value is −30 kJ mol⁻¹.
explanation.simple_explanation: Insert the numbers carefully into ΔG = ΔH − TΔS. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

explanation.principle: Heat release to the surroundings defines an exothermic process.
steps: When the system loses heat, the surroundings gain it.
That is why exothermic reactions often warm their surroundings.
The enthalpy change for such reactions is negative.
Therefore the reaction is exothermic.
explanation.simple_explanation: If heat is released, the reaction is exothermic. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

steps: A spontaneous process can still be slow if its activation energy is high.
It also does not have to be explosive or irreversible.

Therefore the correct answer is thermodynamically favored.

steps: When the system loses heat, the surroundings gain it.
That is why exothermic reactions often warm their surroundings.
The enthalpy change for such reactions is negative.
Therefore the reaction is exothermic.
explanation.simple_explanation: If heat is released, the reaction is exothermic. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: More disorder in a system means

explanation.question_restate: We are to relate disorder to entropy.
explanation.principle: Entropy is a measure of randomness or disorder.
steps: If the arrangement of particles becomes more random, disorder increases.
An increase in disorder means an increase in entropy.
So the system is said to have higher entropy.
Therefore more disorder means higher entropy.
explanation.simple_explanation: More randomness means higher entropy. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: A process with ΔG° < 0 is

explanation.question_restate: We are to classify a process from the sign of ΔG°.
explanation.principle: Negative Gibbs free energy indicates thermodynamic favorability.
steps: If ΔG° is less than zero, the process can occur spontaneously under standard conditions.
This does not necessarily mean it is fast.
It simply means the forward change is favored.
Therefore the process is spontaneous.
explanation.simple_explanation: Negative ΔG° means the process is thermodynamically favored. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

question: A spontaneous change under given conditions is one that is

explanation.question_restate: We are to state the correct meaning of spontaneity.
explanation.principle: Spontaneity refers to favorability, not to the speed of reaction.
steps: A spontaneous process can still be slow if its activation energy is high.
It also does not have to be explosive or irreversible.
What matters is that the process is thermodynamically favored under the conditions.
Therefore the correct answer is thermodynamically favored.
explanation.simple_explanation: Spontaneous means favored by thermodynamics, not necessarily fast. To understand this idea better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Enthalpy changes, (ΔH), in physical and chemical processes
question: A reaction that gives out heat to the surroundings is

explanation.question_restate: We are to name a reaction that releases heat.
